opts.text: Wait until APS investigation is complete + abuse CONFIRMED before reporting + safety plan + restricted visitation; if reporting before confirmation, RN may face civil liability for false accusation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. REASONABLE SUSPICION (not confirmation) is the threshold for mandated reporting per CA WIC §15630. RN is GRANTED IMMUNITY for good-faith reports per §15634. FAILURE to report when reasonable suspicion exists is a MISDEMEANOR with penalties up to 1 yr + $5,000 if death/GBI results. Safety planning + restricted visitation are immediate clinical safety decisions made independently of investigation outcome. Waiting = preventable delay + endangers patient + RN faces non-report penalties.

opts.text: Confront the son directly + demand explanation for the patient's injuries + tell him you will report him to APS unless he confesses + provides version of events; threaten him with legal consequences to motivate cooperation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. RN role is RECOGNIZE + DOCUMENT + REPORT + SUPPORT — NOT interrogate or confront. Confrontation: (1) compromises APS investigation, (2) may escalate danger to patient, (3) interferes with rapport, (4) is not within RN scope of investigation, (5) violates confidentiality of mandated report. INSTEAD: document son's stated history neutrally + report to APS + APS investigates + law enforcement if active danger.

opts.text: Pressure the patient to agree to placement away from son + tell her she must choose her safety over her relationship + threaten that she will be discharged to a nursing home if she does not agree; if patient refuses, she is not capable of decisions and can be overridden
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + multiple ethical violations. (1) Pressure/coercion violates autonomy + trauma-informed care + may revictimize patient; (2) Capacity is decision-specific + cognitive impairment does NOT automatically eliminate capacity — formal assessment required, NOT 'override because she disagrees'; (3) Loyalty/fear/dependence are common reasons capable victims refuse intervention — accept + support + provide resources + monitor; (4) Mandated reporting is non-negotiable but ongoing intervention respects autonomy if capacity present. CORRECT: capacity assessment + APS engagement + safety planning + education + resources + monitoring + patient voice + values respected.

opts.text: Use the daughter as the primary medical interpreter for sensitive abuse-related interviews because she is family, knows the patient, and it expedites communication while saving institutional resources
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. PROFESSIONAL MEDICAL INTERPRETER is required per institutional + Joint Commission standards + best-practice particularly for sensitive interviews. Family members inappropriate due to emotional bias + lack of medical terminology + mistranslation risk + ethical conflict + privacy + role confusion. Daughter participates as FAMILY not interpreter even with medical literacy. Suspected perpetrator never used as interpreter.

opts.text: Multidisciplinary engagement: Social Work (lead coordinator) + Geriatric Medicine + Wound Care + Nutrition + Infectious Disease + PT/OT + Psychiatry + Ethics + Palliative Care + Chaplain + Spanish Interpreter + Pharmacy + Case Management + APS Investigator + Law Enforcement + Elder Law + daughter as collaborative family resource; daily family meetings facilitated by social work
opts.rat: Multidisciplinary coordination essential for complex elder abuse case. Each discipline contributes. Daughter as collaborative family member; son handled per safety plan + APS investigation. Daily family meetings facilitated by social work.

opts.text: OBJECTIVE descriptive documentation: body diagram with annotation; photography per institutional protocol with consent + scale + identifying marker + dated/timed + EMR secure; verbatim quotes in quotation marks; source attribution; history-injury mismatch + timeline; all consults + referrals + reports with date/time/recipient; mandated reporting completion + APS intake number + law enforcement report number; reporter identity confidential per CA WIC §15633.5; AVOID conclusory language
opts.rat: Objective + descriptive documentation supports both patient safety + APS investigation + potential legal proceedings. Body diagram + photography + verbatim + source-attributed + timeline. AVOID conclusory 'appears abused' — DESCRIBE what observed. Confidentiality of reporter identity.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at ED admission
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: STABILIZE acute sepsis: blood + urine cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics (cefepime + vancomycin) within 1 hr of recognition, 30 mL/kg crystalloid resuscitation, MAP ≥65 target with vasopressor PRN, lactate q6h × 24 hr, Foley for source control + I/O, cautious hypernatremia correction (max 10 mEq/L per 24 hr to avoid cerebral edema), REFEEDING precautions (replace phos/K/Mg BEFORE caloric load + thiamine BEFORE carbs)
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Acute medical emergency. Sepsis bundle is time-sensitive. Without medical stabilization, all other concerns are moot. Cautious hypernatremia correction + refeeding precautions concurrent. Foundation for all subsequent care.

opts.text: SEPARATE patient from caregiver + conduct PRIVATE INTERVIEW with professional Spanish interpreter (NOT family); trauma-informed approach + open-ended non-leading questions + direct supportive ask about safety + neutral language; document VERBATIM quotes + history-injury mismatch + observations; full HEAD-TO-TOE assessment with BODY DIAGRAM + PHOTOGRAPHY per institutional protocol with consent
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Concurrent with medical stabilization. Private interview + objective documentation. Professional interpreter not family. Trauma-informed + neutral. Documentation supports both safety + investigation. Time-sensitive while patient is in safe setting + before any pressure or revictimization.

opts.text: MANDATED REPORTING: PHONE to APS within 24 hr (intake number documented); PHONE to LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT for suspected physical abuse with serious injury; WRITTEN SOC 341 within 2 working days; reporter identity confidential; immunity for good-faith reports per CA WIC §15634; document all reports + recipients + intake numbers
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Time-sensitive legal obligation (within 24 hr phone + 2 working days written). Reasonable suspicion is threshold. Multiple reporters each independent. Reporter immunity. Failure-to-report penalties. CRITICAL ETHICAL + LEGAL action.

opts.text: ACTIVATE SAFETY PLAN: son visitation RESTRICTED pending APS investigation (social work explanation to patient + family); security notified + bedside sign + shift report awareness; private room; daughter as collaborative family member supported; protect patient from secondary victimization + escalation
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Immediate safety planning + protection from suspected perpetrator. Hospital safe haven. Restricted visitation pending APS investigation. Multidisciplinary awareness + coordination. Daughter as resource.

opts.text: ENGAGE multidisciplinary team: Social Work (lead coordinator) + Geriatric Medicine + Wound Care + Nutrition + Infectious Disease + PT/OT + Ethics + Palliative + Chaplain + Spanish Interpreter + Pharmacy + Case Management + APS Investigator + Law Enforcement; CAPACITY assessment when stable; daily family meetings facilitated by social work; post-acute planning + caregiver stress addressed for son via APS + supports
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Multidisciplinary coordination essential. Each discipline contributes. Capacity-sensitive engagement + autonomy-protection balance. Daily family meetings. Post-acute planning + caregiver stress addressed for recurrence prevention. Foundation for safe long-term outcome.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: Hospital Day 2 morning: sepsis resolving (HR 82, MAP 83 on no pressors, lactate 1.4, T 36.8°C); hypernatremia correcting slowly (Na 152 → 148 → 144 over 48 hr — appropriate rate ≤10 mEq/L per 24 hr); Cr 2.4 → 1.8 (AKI resolving); refeeding precautions in place + phos + K + Mg replaced + thiamine started + nutrition at 750 kcal/day advancing 10% daily with stable labs; Stage 4 sacral wound cleansed + packed + wound surgery evaluating debridement; APS investigator visited bedside Day 2; safety plan in place
opts.rat: Standard expected trajectory with appropriate sepsis bundle + cautious hypernatremia correction + refeeding precautions + safety plan + APS engagement. Medical stabilization + multidisciplinary engagement working. Foundation for ongoing care + investigation + recovery.

opts.text: Hospital Day 3: APS investigator interviewed patient privately with Spanish interpreter + completed initial investigation; substantiated NEGLECT + PHYSICAL ABUSE; safety plan continued; multidisciplinary case conference held with patient (capacity confirmed by geriatric medicine + ethics — decision-specific capacity present) + daughter + social work + APS + medical team; patient stated values + preferences clearly; daughter willing to take temporary leave from work + relocate to support mother's recovery + post-acute placement; SNF for 4–8 weeks wound healing + nutrition + rehab agreed; long-term placement decision deferred to post-SNF + ongoing APS coordination
opts.rat: Excellent multidisciplinary coordination + patient-centered + capacity-sensitive engagement + daughter as collaborative resource. APS investigation substantiated findings (formalized what clinical team recognized). SNF for recovery + then long-term decision. Patient voice + autonomy respected. Standard expected trajectory.

opts.text: Hospital Day 5: Patient developed acute confusion + agitation + tachycardia + new fevers; bedside concern for sepsis recurrence vs C. diff vs delirium; workup activated — UA + C. diff + cultures + CBC + lactate + ABG; bedside repositioning + reorientation + family at bedside + non-pharmacologic delirium measures; haloperidol 0.5 mg IV PRN for severe agitation only
opts.rat: New acute change requiring workup + management. Multiple possible etiologies in vulnerable patient (sepsis recurrence + C. diff + delirium + medication effects). Bedside workup + non-pharmacologic delirium measures first + low-dose haloperidol PRN only for severe agitation. RN bedside surveillance + escalation critical. New finding = worsening trajectory needs attention.

opts.text: Hospital Day 14: Stage 4 sacral wound debrided + flap reconstruction successful + healing well; nutrition advancing + weight stable + albumin rising to 2.6 + prealbumin 18; sepsis fully resolved; physical therapy progressing + contractures reducing slowly + can transfer to chair with assistance × 30 min; cognitive testing SLUMS 22/30 (mild cognitive impairment baseline confirmed); capacity for healthcare decisions present + values clear; APS substantiated findings + ongoing case management + son's visitation supervised + caregiver stress program engaged + mental health/substance use referral; daughter on extended FMLA + planning to relocate temporarily
opts.rat: Excellent recovery trajectory at Day 14. Wound healing post-flap. Nutrition + functional + cognitive recovery. Capacity confirmed. APS ongoing + son engaged in caregiver support + mental health referral. Daughter committed. Foundation for SNF transition + long-term safe placement.

opts.text: Hospital Day 7: Son arrived at hospital unannounced + agitated + demanding to see his mother + refusing to leave; security called + son escorted off premises + restraining order considered + APS notified; patient distressed at the disturbance + reassured by daughter + RN + chaplain + reorientation; safety plan reinforced; documentation
opts.rat: Active safety event requiring de-escalation + reinforcement of safety plan + law enforcement + APS coordination. Patient distress acknowledged + reassurance + non-pharmacologic support. RN bedside role in safety + advocacy + documentation. Coordinated response prevents harm. New event = worsening needs attention.

opts.text: Day 30 (SNF transition): Patient transferred to SNF for 4–8 weeks wound healing + nutrition + functional rehabilitation; daughter visiting daily + active participation in care + caregiver education; son's supervised visitation at SNF began with caregiver support program engagement; APS ongoing monitoring + court-ordered conservatorship NOT pursued — patient retains decision-making capacity for most decisions; long-term placement plan being developed
opts.rat: Standard expected SNF transition + multidisciplinary engagement + daughter as primary collaborative resource + son's supervised visitation with caregiver support (recovery + recurrence prevention) + APS ongoing monitoring + patient autonomy + capacity respected. No conservatorship needed because patient has decision-specific capacity. Standard recovery trajectory.

opts.text: Month 4 follow-up: Patient discharged from SNF to daughter's home with home health + IHSS caregiver hours + adult day program; sacral wound healed completely; weight back to 49 kg (BMI 19 — still underweight but improving); cognitive stable (SLUMS 22/30 = MCI baseline); attending senior center 3 days/week + medical appointments + family support; son in caregiver support + mental health treatment + supervised visits with mother + working on his own recovery + APS satisfied with progress + daughter primary caregiver + mother safe + thriving
opts.rat: Excellent long-term outcome — meaningful recovery + safe placement + caregiver support for son + APS satisfied + patient autonomy + values respected. Multidisciplinary coordination achieved goals. Demonstrates value of mandated reporting + safety planning + capacity-sensitive engagement + caregiver stress addressed for recurrence prevention. Family healing + son's recovery + patient's thriving = ideal outcome.

opts.text: Hospital Day 4: Capacity assessment by geriatric medicine + ethics consult — patient has DECISION-SPECIFIC CAPACITY for most decisions (understands situation + appreciates consequences + reasons through information + expresses stable choice); baseline mild cognitive impairment + donepezil confirmed; SLUMS 22/30 = MCI; capacity for healthcare decisions + placement decisions + financial decisions present; respects autonomy in ongoing planning; mandated reporting was non-negotiable + made; ongoing decisions made WITH patient + autonomy respected
opts.rat: Capacity confirmed at Day 4 = ongoing baseline state. Patient has decision-specific capacity for most decisions. Mandated reporting was non-negotiable (made). Ongoing decisions respect autonomy. NOT improving or worsening — capacity is a determination + patient values + voice maintained throughout. RN documents capacity finding + decision-making respects patient autonomy.

opts.text: Hospital Day 2: RN proceeds with NO mandated report to APS because the patient stated 'I don't want him in trouble. He's all I have' and the RN determines that the patient is an adult who has the right to refuse intervention + the suspected abuse is a private family matter not requiring outside agency involvement
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + LEGALLY + ETHICALLY UNACCEPTABLE. Mandated reporting is REQUIRED per CA WIC §15630 when reasonable suspicion exists — patient's stated wishes do NOT override mandated reporting obligation (HIPAA exception). RN is a MANDATED REPORTER + failure to report = misdemeanor with penalties up to 1 yr + $5,000 if death/GBI results. Patient autonomy respected in ongoing intervention decisions if capacity present BUT NOT for the reporting itself. Patient is also placed at risk by non-report. CORRECT response: report immediately + respect patient autonomy in ongoing planning + provide education + safety resources.

opts.text: Hospital Day 6: Patient developed pulmonary embolism from prolonged immobility + dehydration + malnutrition cascade despite SCD prophylaxis (no anticoagulation yet given recent severe pressure injury + ongoing wound concern + bleeding risk); IV heparin initiated with careful monitoring; pulmonary intervention coordinated; safety plan + APS investigation continue alongside acute medical management
opts.rat: Acute complication of prolonged immobility + dehydration + severe illness state. PE requires IV anticoagulation balanced against bleeding risk with active wound + planned surgical debridement. Multidisciplinary coordination essential. RN bedside surveillance + escalation. Acute medical management continues alongside abuse investigation + safety planning. New complication = worsening needs attention.
